name: HUMUS 14
tagline: one bowl · six garnishes · warm pita every four minutes
equipment: granite stone mill for fresh-ground chickpeas (Tufi Matana, Turkey)
twin pressure-cookers for overnight ful (100 L each)
wood-burning taboon oven for pita (hand-built by Ramat Gan masons)
tahini-drizzle brass spoons rack (hand-beaten in Jaffa)
ice-well with six copper-lidded garnish bowls
salvaged concrete counter with brass footrail
audience: Florentin locals on lunch break from print-shops and studios
Tel Aviv tech workers escaping high-rise towers
tourists sent by a friend — never by a guidebook
night-shift DJs after Cuckoo's Nest closes
rituals: customer orders one of six garnish styles → ladle of warm hummus poured from the centre → well of olive oil pressed into middle → whole chickpeas + garnish spooned in → pita handed over the counter straight from taboon oven, still steaming
signature_dish: HUMUS MASABACHA — whole warm chickpeas, sunny tahini, split lemon, crushed cumin, smoked olive oil, fresh parsley
unique: the only hummus bar in Tel Aviv that mills chickpeas fresh every morning at 05:30, serves only six garnish styles (never more), and closes the moment the last chickpea is ground
materials: raw concrete counter with brass footrail · glazed terracotta bowls · olive-wood boards · unlacquered brass · hand-thrown clay cups · cream Jaffa stone walls · stained pine shelving
font_pair: Söhne Breit (display, hebrew + latin) · Atlas Grotesk (body)
brandmark: numeral 14 in solid olive-ochre, hand-painted HUMUS below · stamped on kraft takeaway bags with burnt-ochre ink
